project.title: Species identification of silks by protein mass spectrometry
project.projectDescription: Investigating the origins of sericulture is challenging, as classification of silks by species is currently technically difficult. Here we investigate a range of methods for solubilizing modern and archaeological silks and demonstrate a protocol using mass spectrometry-based proteomics to successfully differentiate modern samples of Bombyx, Antheraea, and Samia   -produced silks matching samples to species level. We also analyzed archaeological silks from excavations at the ancient city of Palmyra, Syria, and provide evidence that these silks were produced by A. mylitta, which is the first direct evidence supporting the production and trade of Indian wild silk in antiquity.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: The concentrated fibroin solution was was digested using trypsin or chymotrypsin (1:50) in 50mM ABC for 18-20 hours at 37 ºC. For FA(10%)- trypsin digestion system, a portion of fibroin solution was taken to a separate tube, mixed with formic acid (10% to volume) and set 4hrs to overnight before trypsin digestion. Then, acetonitrile (ACN) was added to the filter unit up to 80% of volume with enzyme (1:100) and further digested for 4 more hours.  In case of chymotrypsin-trypsin digestion, the sample was first digested with chymotrypsin, then trypsin was added with ACN. Digestion process was stopped by adding formic acid to 5% of volume. The resulted peptides were vacuum dried, eluted with 0.1% formic acid (FA) for the peptide cleaning using 100µl size ZipTip (C18, Thermo), followed by the steps described below. Peptides were extracted in 80% CAN in 0.1% FA buffer, vacuum dried and resuspended in elution buffer for LC-MS analysis.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: .  The peptides were analyzed on a NanoAcquity-UPLC system (Waters) connected to an Orbitrap Elite mass spectrometer (Thermo Fischer Scientific) possessing an EASY-Spray nano-electrospray ion source (Thermo Fischer Scientific). The peptides were trapped on an in-house packed guard column (75 μm i.d. x 20 mm, Acclaim PepMap C18, 3μm, 100 Å) using solvent A (0.1% Formic Acid in water) at a pressure of 140 bar. The peptides were separated on an EASY-spray Acclaim PepMap® analytical column (75 μm i.d. × 50 mm, RSLC C18, 3 μm, 100 Å) using a linear gradient (length: 100 minutes, 3 % to 60 % solvent B (0.1% formic acid in acetonitrile), flow rate: 300 nL/min). The separated peptides were electro-sprayed directly into the mass spectrometer operating in a data-dependent mode using a CID based method. Full scan MS spectra (scan range 350-1500 m/z, resolution 120000, AGC target 1e6, maximum injection time 250 ms) and subsequent CID MS/MS spectra (AGC target 5e4, maximum injection time 100 ms) of 10 most intense peaks were acquired in the Ion Trap. CID fragmentation was performed at 35 % of normalized collision energy and the signal intensity threshold was kept at 500 counts. The CID method used performs beam-type CID fragmentation of the peptides.

references.referenceLine: Lee B, Pires E, Pollard AM, McCullagh JSO. Species identification of silks by protein mass spectrometry reveals evidence of wild silk use in antiquity. Sci Rep. 2022 12(1):4579

Characteristics[NumberOfSamples]: Five fibre samples were collected from three alleged silk textiles found at the archaeological site of ancient Palmyra (Fig. 5a).
Characteristics[Specimen]: Textile S8 was made of very shiny, flat fibres, and only the weft had slight torsion in the Z direction based on previous microscopic analysis.
Comment[Instrument]: Orbitrap Elite mass spectrometer (Thermo Fischer Scientific) possessing an EASY-Spray nano-electrospray ion source (Thermo Fischer Scientific)
Comment[Separation]: nanoAcquity-UPLC system (Waters) connected to an Orbitrap Elite mass spectrometer (Thermo Fischer Scientific)
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: operating in a data-dependent mode using a CID based method
Comment[FragmentationMethod]: CID fragmentation was performed at 35% of normalized collision energy
Comment[IonizationType]: EASY-Spray nano-electrospray ion source (Thermo Fischer Scientific)
Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance]: Precursor mass tolerance was set as 15 ppm
Comment[FragmentMassTolerance]: fragment mass tolerances for CID were set to 0.5 Da

Characteristics[NumberOfSamples]: Five fibre samples were collected from three alleged silk textiles found at the archaeological site of ancient Palmyra (Fig. 5a).
Characteristics[Specimen]: Textiles S48 and S49 had similar looking flat fibres for both warp and weft.
